text.title: Tool-Tier Approval Gate Core Rules
text.summary: 9 testable rules with rationale + source for inc-tool-tier-approval-gate. Includes terminal skip-this-methodology rule.

rule.statement: Tool tiers MUST be exactly four: T0 (read-only), T1 (mutable-self), T2 (customer-impact), T3 (multi-cluster / cross-tenant); custom tiers are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Reducing the policy surface to four tiers prevents endless 'is this T1.5?' debates.

rule.statement: Every tool in the catalog MUST carry an explicit tier; absence defaults to T3 (most restrictive).
rule.rationale: Default-strict prevents tools sneaking in at T0.

rule.statement: T0 tools execute freely; outputs are still captured to audit log for post-incident review.
rule.rationale: Reads are safe but evidence of agent reasoning is essential to post-incident reconstruction.

rule.statement: T1 tools (e.g. write to a personal scratchpad, restart agent's own sidecar) execute without approval but emit audit log entry.
T2 tools (e.g. deploy a service, rotate a customer secret) MUST present a signed approval token bound to action class.
T3 tools (e.g. cluster-wide change, cross-tenant migration) MUST require a two-person rule + ≥5-minute cooling period after first approval.
rule.rationale: NUREG / NIST high-trust controls — two-person rule + cooling cap human-error and panic decisions.

rule.statement: Promoting a tool from a higher tier to a lower tier MUST go through code review with security signoff.
rule.rationale: Demotion is the threat; review prevents stealth liberalisation.

rule.statement: Emergency tier-bypass MUST emit a high-severity audit entry; security team reviews same day.
rule.rationale: Bypass without review becomes the new default.

rule.statement: If agent has only 1-2 tools or existing flat RBAC is adequate, return `skipped`.
